config.purpose: localize the piecewise_kmeans m=4 first-few-epochs slowdown on LRA ListOps (A100, bf16, seq 2000): the decay is a bf16 backward cost dominated by scatter_add_ (the backward of the per-query anchor gathers), not clustering, the second-moment einsum, data page-cache, allocator growth, or k-means convergence

per_epoch_compute_seconds_from_prior_uni.note: results/lra/listops/results.json (10-epoch bf16 run). compute_time per epoch; data_time flat ~1.3s throughout; all other methods flat across epochs.

fixed_batch_fwdbwd_ms_by_training_step.note: same batch timed at increasing training steps; the cost does NOT drop within epoch 1 (it rises to step 300), so the epoch-scale recovery (2590->1293 s at epoch 1->2) is a multi-epoch effect, not an early-training transient

attention_sharpness_control.note: scaling q_proj weights x1/4/16 to sharpen attention alpha does NOT reduce fwd+bwd -> refutes attention-entropy as the decay driver

profiler_top_cuda_ops_bf16_fwdbwd_at_ini.role: backward of torch.gather (anchor selection); atomic-add high-collision (2048 positions -> 4 anchor slots)
